name: Gravatar API Vocabulary
description: Key terms and concepts used in the Gravatar Public API

terms.definition: A SHA256 hash of a lowercase, trimmed email address used to identify a Gravatar user. Used as the primary identifier for avatar and profile lookups.
A Gravatar user profile containing display name, location, job title, verified accounts, interests, links, and other identity information tied to an email address.
A user-uploaded profile image associated with a Gravatar account. Avatars have ratings (G, PG, R, X) and can be set per email address.
A content rating applied to avatars and profiles. Values: G (suitable for all audiences), PG (parental guidance), R (restricted), X (adult content).
A third-party account (e.g., GitHub, Twitter/X, Mastodon) that a Gravatar user has verified ownership of, displayed on their public profile.
A generated QR code image that links to a Gravatar profile, identified by SHA256 hash of the user's email address.
A value used to look up a Gravatar profile: either a SHA256 hash of the email address or a profile slug (username).
A topic or hobby listed on a Gravatar profile, with an ID, name, and URL slug.
A cryptocurrency wallet address listed on a Gravatar profile, enabling crypto donations (e.g., Bitcoin, Ethereum).
An image in the Gravatar gallery associated with a user profile, distinct from the currently selected avatar.
An external URL listed on a Gravatar profile, such as a personal website or social media page.
A language associated with a Gravatar profile, represented by code and name.
The response object returned when associating an email address with an avatar.
An API key used for authenticating requests to the Gravatar REST API, passed as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.
A unique identifier for a specific avatar image uploaded to Gravatar, used to manage and reference individual avatar images.
A hash identifying a specific avatar image, used in avatar management endpoints.
